title: Value Network: Lessors of Nonresidential Buildings (NAICS 531120)

sections.title: Metadata
sections.content:
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| NAICS Code | 531120 |
| NAICS Title | Lessors of Nonresidential Buildings (except Miniwarehouses) |
| NAICS Sector | 53 — Real Estate and Rental and Leasing |
| Industry Type | Service |
| Process VN | YES |
| Product BOM | NO (service NAICS — outputs are occupancy outcomes, not physical products) |
| Market Size (fire/safety systems, US+EU) | ~$600M est. [[SRC: 05_market_discovery §Market04]]; global fire protection market $75–85B incl. all sectors [[SRC: MarketsandMarkets/Mordor 2025]] |
| TAM (sector, global) | ~$1.8T [[SRC: NAICS 531120 market research, Grata/IBISWorld]] |
| PropTech spend | $45.7B globally in 2025, ~55% commercial [[SRC: Coherent Market Insights 2025]] |

---

sections.title: Phase 1 — A.1: L6 Core Process Steps
sections.content:
**CFJ (industry-level):** Provide leasable nonresidential building space to tenant organizations while maintaining regulatory-compliant safety infrastructure and preserving long-term asset value.

| ID | Name | Job Family | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| L6a | Property Acquisition | Identify and capitalize commercial real estate assets for the portfolio | Due diligence, valuation, debt/equity sourcing |
| L6b | Asset Preparation | Prepare building systems and access points for occupant use | MEP fit-out, safety-critical hardware installation |
| L6c | Tenant Acquisition | Market vacancies, qualify tenants, and execute lease agreements | CRE listing, screening, lease execution |
| L6d | Building Operations | Operate and maintain physical building systems for continuous safe occupancy | **Primary step where Emergency Exit Security sits** |
| L6e | Tenant Services | Service active tenants, manage obligations, handle renewals and exits | Helpdesk, experience platforms, work orders |

Core steps numbered in operational sequence: L6a → L6b → L6c → L6d (ongoing) || L6e (ongoing).

---

sections.title: Phase 1 — A.2: L6 Horizontal Functions
sections.content:
| ID | Name (H) | Runs Across | Distinction from Core |
|---|---|---|---|
| L6H1 | Regulatory & Compliance (H) | All L6 core steps | Does not produce space — enables all steps to operate legally; fire safety inspections span L6b–L6d continuously |
| L6H2 | Financial Management (H) | All L6 core steps | Rent collection, NOI reporting, debt service; prerequisite for every step but transforms no space |

> **Warning test applied:** Removing L6d (Building Operations) means building cannot be maintained → output cannot be delivered. Core. Removing L6H1 (Compliance) means the output is produced illegally → cannot run safely/legally. Horizontal. Test passes.

---

sections.title: Phase 1 — A.3: L5 Equipment per L6 Node
sections.content:
### L6a — Property Acquisition

| L5 | Alternatives | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Commercial Valuation Analytics | 1. Enterprise ARGUS/MSCI (~45%) · 2. CoStar Analytics (~25%) · 3. Custom Excel/DCF model (~30%) | — | MEDIUM | Analyst estimates, CRE industry surveys |
| Due Diligence Management | 1. SaaS VDR platforms (Datasite, Intralinks) (~55%) · 2. Legal matter management software (~25%) · 3. General document management (~20%) | — | LOW | Expert inference |

### L6b — Asset Preparation

| L5 | Alternatives | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Building Mechanical Systems | 1. Integrated HVAC (central chiller/AHU plant) (~50%) · 2. VRF/split systems (~30%) · 3. District cooling/heating (~20%) | — | MEDIUM | ASHRAE market data |
| Electrical Infrastructure | 1. Standard LV distribution (~70%) · 2. Smart metering w/ sub-tenant allocation (~20%) · 3. Microgrid/on-site generation (~10%) | — | MEDIUM | Energy market surveys |

### L6c — Tenant Acquisition

| L5 | Alternatives | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Lease Management Platform | 1. Enterprise IWMS (Yardi, MRI, RealPage) (~55%) · 2. Mid-market property management SaaS (~30%) · 3. Spreadsheet/legacy (~15% declining) | — | MEDIUM | PropTech market reports 2025 |
| CRE Listing & Marketing | 1. CoStar/LoopNet (~50%) · 2. JLL/CBRE broker portal (~30%) · 3. Own website / direct (~20%) | — | LOW | Expert inference |

### L6d — Building Operations ⟵ Primary branch for Türwächter

| L5 | Alternatives | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Building Management System (BMS)** | 1. Integrated BMS platform (Siemens Desigo, JCI Metasys, Honeywell EBI) (~50%) · 2. Point solutions / standalone HVAC+lighting controls (~30%) · 3. Cloud-native BMS/BAS SaaS (~20% growing) | — | MEDIUM | PropTech / IBMS market data 2025 |
| **Fire Detection & Alarm** | 1. Addressable fire alarm systems (~55%) · 2. Conventional fire alarm panels (legacy, ~30%) · 3. Wireless fire detection systems (~15% growing) | — | HIGH | Multiple fire protection market reports 2025 |
| **Emergency Exit Security** ← Türwächter L5 | 1. Mechanical door alarm guards, no connectivity (~40%) · 2. **Wireless IoT exit alarm guards** (~20%) ← Türwächter category · 3. Wired electronic exit control, alarm-panel-integrated (~25%) · 4. Integrated alarmed panic hardware (~15%) | — | LOW | No published sub-segment data; expert inference from access control market structure |
| **Access Control** | 1. Mobile credential / cloud ACaaS (~40% growing) · 2. Smart card / proximity card (~35%) · 3. PIN keypad entry (~15%) · 4. Traditional mechanical key (~10% declining) | — | MEDIUM | MarketsandMarkets 2025; Grand View Research |

### L6e — Tenant Services

| L5 | Alternatives | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Tenant Experience Platform | 1. Dedicated PropTech tenancy apps (Equiem, HqO) (~35%) · 2. BMS-integrated dashboard (~30%) · 3. Email/portal-based self-service (~35%) | — | LOW | PropTech adoption surveys; >70% of institutional RE firms use such platforms [[SRC: IMARC/Coherent 2025]] |

### L6H1 — Regulatory & Compliance (H)

| L5 | Alternatives | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Fire Safety Inspection Management | 1. Integrated FM compliance platform (ServiceNow, Corrigo) (~40%) · 2. Standalone inspection software (~25%) · 3. Paper-based / spreadsheet logs (~35% declining) | — | LOW | Industry observation; compliance management market trends |

### L6H2 — Financial Management (H)

| L5 | Alternatives | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Property Accounting Platform | 1. Yardi Voyager / MRI Financial (~60%) · 2. SAP/Oracle RE modules (~20%) · 3. General-purpose accounting (QuickBooks, Xero) (~20%) | — | MEDIUM | CRE software market reports |

---

sections.title: Phase 1 — A.4: Output Type Activation Matrix
sections.content:
Output types = building/tenant categories managed under NAICS 531120.

| L6 Step | Class A Office | Class B/C Office | Retail Commercial | Industrial Flex | Medical Office |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| L6a Property Acquisition | x | x | x | x | x |
| L6b Asset Preparation | x | x | x | x | x |
| L6c Tenant Acquisition | x | x | x | x | x |
| L6d Building Operations | x | x | x | x | x |
| L6e Tenant Services | x | x | — | — | x |
| L6H1 Compliance | x | x | x | x | x |
| L6H2 Financial | x | x | x | x | x |

> **Column read:** Medical Office activates all 6 L6 nodes including Tenant Services (high tenant-relationship intensity). Industrial Flex skips Tenant Services — tenants are typically self-managing.
> **Row read:** L6d Building Operations is the only step where Emergency Exit Security lives; it activates universally across all output types — every building type must comply with fire safety law.

---

sections.title: Phase 2 — 04a: L4 Fill (≥ 3 L4 per L5)
sections.content:
Only critical branches fully decomposed. All L5 nodes meet ≥ 2 L4 minimum.

### L6d — Building Management System

| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| HVAC Control Module | PLC/DDC unit for heating, cooling, ventilation setpoints |
| Lighting Control | Relay/dimmer control panels; occupancy sensor integration |
| Energy Meter | kWh sub-meters per tenant zone; enables utility billing and ESG reporting |

### L6d — Fire Detection & Alarm

| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| Smoke & Heat Detector | Addressable point detectors; optical/ionization or combined |
| Alarm Panel & Controller | Central FACP; maps all detector/zone addresses; triggers outputs |
| Notification Appliance | Horn/strobe devices per floor; audible and visual evacuation signal |

### L6d — Emergency Exit Security ⟵ Türwächter fits here as L4[1]

| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| **Door Guardian Alarm Unit** | Mechanical guard housing mounted over door handle; triggers 95 dB alarm on activation; battery-powered. **← Türwächter (GfS Exit Control 179/1125)** |
| Wireless Monitoring Receiver | 868 MHz / BLE receiver collecting door-state and alarm events from guardian units; potential-free relay output to BMS |
| BUS Gateway & Aggregator | Centralized hub aggregating multi-unit status; relays events to building dashboard or BMS integration layer |
| Signal Repeater | RF range extender (~+30 m per stage); required in buildings >30 m from nearest receiver |

### L6d — Access Control

| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| Credential Reader | Card/mobile/biometric reader at door; interfaces to controller |
| Door Controller | Edge device governing lock release, audit logging, and alarm output |
| Identity Management Backend | Cloud or on-premise user directory, access policy engine, and audit log store |

### L6c — Lease Management Platform

| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| Lease Abstraction Engine | NLP/OCR layer parsing lease documents into structured fields |
| Rent Roll & Billing | Scheduled charge generation, tenant invoicing, arrears tracking |
| Tenant Screening Module | Credit, background, and reference check integration |

### L6H1 — Fire Safety Inspection Management

| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| Inspection Scheduling Engine | Calendar-driven task assignments for door checks, extinguisher, and alarm tests |
| Audit Trail & Document Archive | Timestamped records of all safety hardware inspections; exportable for fire marshal audits |
| Regulatory Calendar | Jurisdiction-specific compliance deadlines (annual inspections, CE recertification, DIN EN 179/1125 checks) |

### Remaining L5 nodes (≥ 2 L4 each)

| L5 | L4 — 1 | L4 — 2 |
|---|---|---|
| Commercial Valuation Analytics | DCF Modeling Engine | Market Comparable Database |
| Due Diligence Management | Environmental Assessment | Title & Legal Review Tracker |
| Building Mechanical Systems | Air Handling Unit | Chiller / Heat Pump |
| Electrical Infrastructure | Main Distribution Board | Emergency Power Generator |
| CRE Listing & Marketing | Listing Portal Integration | Lead & Pipeline CRM |
| Property Accounting Platform | GL & Chart of Accounts | Rent Collection & Receivables |
| Tenant Experience Platform | Mobile App Client | Service Request Portal |

---

sections.title: Phase 2 — 04b: Completeness Check
sections.content:
| Check Dimension | Present | Missing / Gap |
|---|---|---|
| Core process steps (L6) | L6a–L6e | L6f Asset Disposition omitted (portfolio recycling, sale) — low relevance to Türwächter; acceptable gap |
| Horizontal functions | Compliance, Financial | PropTech/IoT infrastructure absent as horizontal — relevant for Türwächter IoT layer; **suggest adding L6H3 IoT Infrastructure (H)** |
| Emergency exit chain L5→L4 | ✓ complete | — |
| Fire safety regulatory chain | ✓ in L6H1 | — |
| Building automation | ✓ BMS L5 with L4 | — |
| Major domain gaps | None critical | Elevator/vertical transport (L5 under L6d) absent; not relevant for Türwächter scope |

> **is_complete:** false (soft) | **completeness_score:** 0.82 | Missing: L6H3 IoT Infrastructure (H); L6f Asset Disposition; Elevator under L6d.

---

sections.title: Phase 2 — 04c: Naming Review
sections.content:
| Current Name | Action | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| BUS Gateway & Aggregator | Keep | Industry term "BUS" used in GfS documentation and building automation domain |
| Building Management System (BMS) | Keep — drop "(BMS)" in JSON | Parenthetical acceptable in doc; JSON name = "Building Management System" |
| Wireless Monitoring Receiver | Keep | Industry-standard term; GfS uses this exact label |
| Fire Safety Inspection Management | Keep | Domain-standard phrasing in FM/compliance software market |
| Property Accounting Platform | Keep | Standard CRE industry term |

No renames required. All L5/L4 names pass the three-question test:
1. Commonly used in industry? ✓
2. Removing "system"/"module" keeps meaning? ✓ (already minimal)
3. Domain expert recognizes immediately? ✓

---

sections.title: Phase 2 — 04d: Structural Validation (Deterministic)
sections.content:
| Check | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Valid levels | PASS | All units use L7, L6, L5, or L4 — no invalid levels |
| No orphan nodes | PASS | Every non-L7 unit has a valid parent |
| Valid transitions | PASS | L7→L6, L6→L5, L5→L4 only (service NAICS; no L6a/L6b sub-SoS needed) |
| L4 children per L5 ≥ 2 | PASS | All L5 nodes have ≥ 2 L4 children |
| Unit count | 44 units | Soft target 20–40; 44 is within acceptable 15–60 range (Q7 soft pass) |

---

sections.title: Türwächter VN Anchor
sections.content:
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **VN Anchor Level** | L4 |
| **L4 Node** | Door Guardian Alarm Unit |
| **Parent L5** | Emergency Exit Security |
| **Parent L6** | Building Operations (L6d) |
| **CFJ Contribution** | Fulfills the fire-code compliance sub-job within L6d: ensuring emergency exit doors are not misused while remaining evacuation-ready |
| **Türwächter vs. alternatives** | Wireless IoT exit alarm guard (~20% share) — sits between purely mechanical (lower cost, no monitoring) and wired electronic (higher cost, complex install); differentiated by retrofit-ready wireless monitoring without mains power |
| **Constraint overlay** | C3 (range limit) and C6 (EasyWave lock-in) most relevant in this market; large multi-building portfolios need BUS gateway + receiver infrastructure; protocol integration with BMS is the primary sales friction |
| **Downstream step** | This VN anchor feeds Step 05 (VN Anchor & Job Derivation) → job map for the commercial building lessor context |

---

sections.title: QA Checklist
sections.content:
| # | Check | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Q1 | CFJ is industry-level | PASS | "Provide leasable nonresidential building space… while maintaining regulatory-compliant safety infrastructure" — no product/technology reference |
| Q2 | No orphan nodes | PASS | All units reference valid parent |
| Q3 | Valid level transitions | PASS | L7→L6→L5→L4 only; no skipping |
| Q4 | L4 children per L5 ≥ 2 | PASS | Minimum 2 confirmed for all 14 L5 nodes |
| Q5 | No solution-specific L7/L6 names | PASS | "Commercial Property Leasing Ecosystem" (L7); L6 names describe operational phases, not technologies |
| Q6 | Three-question naming test (5 spot checks) | PASS | Building Management System, Emergency Exit Security, Alarm Panel, Credential Reader, Lease Abstraction Engine — all domain-standard |
| Q7 | Unit count 15–60 | SOFT PASS | 44 units (target 20–40; exceeds soft cap but within acceptable range) |

---

rows.Job Family: Operate and maintain physical building systems for continuous safe occupancy
rows.Notes: **Primary step where Emergency Exit Security sits**

rows.Name: Tenant Services
rows.Job Family: Service active tenants, manage obligations, handle renewals and exits

rows.Distinction from Core: Does not produce space — enables all steps to operate legally; fire safety inspections span L6b–L6d continuously
Rent collection, NOI reporting, debt service; prerequisite for every step but transforms no space

rows.Alternatives: 1. Enterprise ARGUS/MSCI (~45%) · 2. CoStar Analytics (~25%) · 3. Custom Excel/DCF model (~30%)
1. SaaS VDR platforms (Datasite, Intralinks) (~55%) · 2. Legal matter management software (~25%) · 3. General document management (~20%)
1. Integrated HVAC (central chiller/AHU plant) (~50%) · 2. VRF/split systems (~30%) · 3. District cooling/heating (~20%)
1. Standard LV distribution (~70%) · 2. Smart metering w/ sub-tenant allocation (~20%) · 3. Microgrid/on-site generation (~10%)
1. Enterprise IWMS (Yardi, MRI, RealPage) (~55%) · 2. Mid-market property management SaaS (~30%) · 3. Spreadsheet/legacy (~15% declining)
1. CoStar/LoopNet (~50%) · 2. JLL/CBRE broker portal (~30%) · 3. Own website / direct (~20%)
1. Integrated BMS platform (Siemens Desigo, JCI Metasys, Honeywell EBI) (~50%) · 2. Point solutions / standalone HVAC+lighting controls (~30%) · 3. Cloud-native BMS/BAS SaaS (~20% growing)
1. Addressable fire alarm systems (~55%) · 2. Conventional fire alarm panels (legacy, ~30%) · 3. Wireless fire detection systems (~15% growing)

1. Mechanical door alarm guards, no connectivity (~40%) · 2. **Wireless IoT exit alarm guards** (~20%) ← Türwächter category · 3. Wired electronic exit control, alarm-panel-integrated (~25%) · 4. Integrated alarmed panic hardware (~15%)
rows.Source: No published sub-segment data; expert inference from access control market structure

rows.Alternatives: 1. Mobile credential / cloud ACaaS (~40% growing) · 2. Smart card / proximity card (~35%) · 3. PIN keypad entry (~15%) · 4. Traditional mechanical key (~10% declining)
1. Dedicated PropTech tenancy apps (Equiem, HqO) (~35%) · 2. BMS-integrated dashboard (~30%) · 3. Email/portal-based self-service (~35%)
rows.Source: PropTech adoption surveys; >70% of institutional RE firms use such platforms [[SRC: IMARC/Coherent 2025]]

rows.Alternatives: 1. Integrated FM compliance platform (ServiceNow, Corrigo) (~40%) · 2. Standalone inspection software (~25%) · 3. Paper-based / spreadsheet logs (~35% declining)
rows.Source: Industry observation; compliance management market trends

rows.Alternatives: 1. Yardi Voyager / MRI Financial (~60%) · 2. SAP/Oracle RE modules (~20%) · 3. General-purpose accounting (QuickBooks, Xero) (~20%)

rows.Description: PLC/DDC unit for heating, cooling, ventilation setpoints
Relay/dimmer control panels; occupancy sensor integration
kWh sub-meters per tenant zone; enables utility billing and ESG reporting
Addressable point detectors; optical/ionization or combined
Central FACP; maps all detector/zone addresses; triggers outputs
Horn/strobe devices per floor; audible and visual evacuation signal
Mechanical guard housing mounted over door handle; triggers 95 dB alarm on activation; battery-powered. **← Türwächter (GfS Exit Control 179/1125)**
868 MHz / BLE receiver collecting door-state and alarm events from guardian units; potential-free relay output to BMS
Centralized hub aggregating multi-unit status; relays events to building dashboard or BMS integration layer
RF range extender (~+30 m per stage); required in buildings >30 m from nearest receiver
Card/mobile/biometric reader at door; interfaces to controller
Edge device governing lock release, audit logging, and alarm output
Cloud or on-premise user directory, access policy engine, and audit log store
NLP/OCR layer parsing lease documents into structured fields
Scheduled charge generation, tenant invoicing, arrears tracking
Credit, background, and reference check integration
Calendar-driven task assignments for door checks, extinguisher, and alarm tests
Timestamped records of all safety hardware inspections; exportable for fire marshal audits
Jurisdiction-specific compliance deadlines (annual inspections, CE recertification, DIN EN 179/1125 checks)

rows.Missing / Gap: L6f Asset Disposition omitted (portfolio recycling, sale) — low relevance to Türwächter; acceptable gap
PropTech/IoT infrastructure absent as horizontal — relevant for Türwächter IoT layer; **suggest adding L6H3 IoT Infrastructure (H)**
Elevator/vertical transport (L5 under L6d) absent; not relevant for Türwächter scope

rows.Reason: Industry term "BUS" used in GfS documentation and building automation domain
Parenthetical acceptable in doc; JSON name = "Building Management System"
Industry-standard term; GfS uses this exact label
Domain-standard phrasing in FM/compliance software market

rows.Notes: All units use L7, L6, L5, or L4 — no invalid levels
Every non-L7 unit has a valid parent
L7→L6, L6→L5, L5→L4 only (service NAICS; no L6a/L6b sub-SoS needed)
All L5 nodes have ≥ 2 L4 children
Soft target 20–40; 44 is within acceptable 15–60 range (Q7 soft pass)

rows.Value: Fulfills the fire-code compliance sub-job within L6d: ensuring emergency exit doors are not misused while remaining evacuation-ready

Wireless IoT exit alarm guard (~20% share) — sits between purely mechanical (lower cost, no monitoring) and wired electronic (higher cost, complex install); differentiated by retrofit-ready wireless monitoring without mains power

C3 (range limit) and C6 (EasyWave lock-in) most relevant in this market; large multi-building portfolios need BUS gateway + receiver infrastructure; protocol integration with BMS is the primary sales friction
This VN anchor feeds Step 05 (VN Anchor & Job Derivation) → job map for the commercial building lessor context

rows.Notes: "Provide leasable nonresidential building space… while maintaining regulatory-compliant safety infrastructure" — no product/technology reference
Minimum 2 confirmed for all 14 L5 nodes
"Commercial Property Leasing Ecosystem" (L7); L6 names describe operational phases, not technologies
Building Management System, Emergency Exit Security, Alarm Panel, Credential Reader, Lease Abstraction Engine — all domain-standard
44 units (target 20–40; exceeds soft cap but within acceptable range)

networks.cfj_statement: Provide leasable nonresidential building space to tenant organizations while maintaining regulatory-compliant safety infrastructure and preserving long-term asset value

units.name: Commercial Property Leasing Ecosystem
units.description: Economic community of building owners, tenants, FM providers, and regulators coordinated toward continuous nonresidential occupancy

units.name: Property Acquisition
units.description: Identify, evaluate, and capitalize commercial real estate assets for the portfolio

units.name: Asset Preparation
units.description: Prepare building systems and access points for tenant occupancy

units.name: Tenant Acquisition
units.description: Market vacancies, qualify tenants, and execute lease agreements

units.name: Building Operations
units.description: Operate and maintain physical building systems for continuous safe occupancy

units.name: Tenant Services
units.description: Service active tenants, manage obligations, handle renewals and exits

units.name: Regulatory & Compliance (H)
units.description: Horizontal: fire safety, building codes, accessibility, environmental compliance runs continuously across all core steps

units.name: Financial Management (H)
units.description: Horizontal: rent collection, NOI reporting, investor distributions, and debt service run continuously

units.name: Commercial Valuation Analytics
units.description: Platforms for DCF modeling, market comparables, and acquisition scenario analysis

units.name: Due Diligence Management
units.description: Virtual data room and task management for environmental, legal, and structural due diligence

units.name: Building Mechanical Systems
units.description: HVAC generation and distribution equipment installed during asset preparation

units.name: Electrical Infrastructure
units.description: LV distribution, metering, and backup power systems

units.name: Lease Management Platform
units.description: IWMS/ERP for lease abstraction, rent roll, billing, and tenant screening

units.name: CRE Listing & Marketing
units.description: Commercial listing portals and broker relationship management tools

units.name: Building Management System
units.description: Integrated BMS/BAS platform for HVAC, lighting, and energy control

units.name: Fire Detection & Alarm
units.description: Addressable fire detection, alarm panels, and notification appliances

units.name: Emergency Exit Security
units.description: Devices securing emergency exit doors against unauthorized use while maintaining unimpeded evacuation capability; includes mechanical alarm guards, wireless IoT variants, and wired electronic exit controls

units.name: Access Control
units.description: Credential-based door control systems: mobile/card readers, controllers, and identity backend

units.name: Tenant Experience Platform
units.description: Tenant-facing mobile apps and portals for service requests, amenity booking, and communications

units.name: Fire Safety Inspection Management
units.description: Compliance software for scheduling, documenting, and archiving fire safety hardware inspections

units.name: Property Accounting Platform
units.description: CRE-specific general ledger, rent collection, and investor reporting

units.name: DCF Modeling Engine
units.description: Discounted cash flow and levered IRR calculation engine for acquisition underwriting

units.name: Market Comparable Database
units.description: Structured store of comparable sale and lease transactions used for valuation benchmarking

units.name: Environmental Assessment
units.description: Phase I/II environmental site assessment records and remediation tracking

units.name: Title & Legal Review Tracker
units.description: Task and document management for title search, encumbrances, and legal sign-off

units.name: Air Handling Unit
units.description: Central HVAC air handler circulating conditioned air to building zones

units.name: Chiller / Heat Pump
units.description: Compression-cycle or absorption chiller for central cooling plant

units.name: Main Distribution Board
units.description: Primary LV switchboard distributing power to building sub-circuits

units.name: Emergency Power Generator
units.description: Diesel or gas standby generator for life-safety and critical systems

units.name: Lease Abstraction Engine
units.description: NLP/OCR module extracting key data fields from lease documents

units.name: Rent Roll & Billing
units.description: Scheduled charge generation, tenant invoicing, and arrears tracking

units.name: Listing Portal Integration
units.description: API connections to CoStar, LoopNet, and broker portals for vacancy syndication

units.name: Lead & Pipeline CRM
units.description: Tenant prospect tracking, tour scheduling, and deal pipeline management

units.name: HVAC Control Module
units.description: DDC/PLC unit executing HVAC setpoint logic; interfaces to building automation network

units.name: Lighting Control
units.description: Relay/dimmer panel and occupancy sensor integration for zone lighting schedules

units.name: Energy Meter
units.description: kWh sub-meters per tenant zone enabling utility cost allocation and ESG reporting

units.name: Smoke & Heat Detector
units.description: Addressable optical/ionization detector; reports to FACP with zone addressing

units.name: Alarm Panel & Controller
units.description: Central fire alarm control panel mapping all detector addresses and triggering outputs

units.name: Notification Appliance
units.description: Horn/strobe devices per floor providing audible and visual evacuation signaling

units.name: Door Guardian Alarm Unit
units.description: Mechanical guard housing over door handle; triggers 95 dB alarm on activation without mains power. GfS Exit Control 179/1125 (Türwächter) is a product instance in this category.

units.name: Wireless Monitoring Receiver
units.description: 868 MHz / BLE receiver collecting door-state and alarm events from guardian units; provides potential-free relay output to BMS

units.name: BUS Gateway & Aggregator
units.description: Centralized hub aggregating multi-unit door status; relays to building dashboard or BMS integration layer

units.name: Signal Repeater
units.description: RF range extender (~+30 m per stage) for buildings exceeding integrated transmitter range

units.name: Credential Reader
units.description: Card/mobile/biometric reader at door entry point; interfaces to door controller

units.name: Door Controller
units.description: Edge device governing lock release, access audit logging, and alarm output per door

units.name: Identity Management Backend
units.description: Cloud or on-premise user directory, access policy engine, and audit log store

units.name: Mobile App Client
units.description: Tenant-facing iOS/Android app for service requests, amenity booking, and communications

units.name: Service Request Portal
units.description: Web/app-based work order submission and status tracking for tenant maintenance requests

units.name: Inspection Scheduling Engine
units.description: Calendar-driven task assignments for fire door checks, extinguisher tests, and alarm testing

units.name: Audit Trail & Document Archive
units.description: Timestamped records of all safety hardware inspections; exportable for fire marshal and insurance audits

units.name: Regulatory Calendar
units.description: Jurisdiction-specific compliance deadline tracking for DIN EN 179/1125, CE re-certification, and building code cycles

units.name: GL & Chart of Accounts
units.description: CRE-configured general ledger with property-level cost center structure

units.name: Rent Collection & Receivables
units.description: Automated direct debit, bank reconciliation, and tenant arrears management
